summary: McKenzie documents reaching feature completeness on his bingo card software, focusing on a frustrating 6.5-hour debugging session fixing text truncation in Java Swing's JLabel component. The bug surfaced when long Japanese/English words overflowed fixed-width containers in print output. He details the technical solution involving custom LayoutManager subclasses and dynamic font resizing. He reflects on whether spending four hours fixing edge cases in a freebie data set was worth it, concluding that the polish differentiates him from competitors and enables more promotional content.
key_points: Java Swing silently truncates text in JLabels without any notification or change to getText(), making the bug only visible in rendered or printed output
The fix required overriding LayoutManager.layoutContainer to dynamically check FontMetrics and resize fonts when text exceeds component width
Japanese-to-English localization creates particularly challenging string width assumptions due to dramatic differences in character physical widths
Spending four hours on edge cases in a freebie data set was justified because it adds polish competitors lack and enables more promotional content
Having a virtual PDF printer saved enormous waste during the iterative debugging of print output issues
money_quotes: Fixing two little edge cases in one little data set in a freebie I'm giving to customers took me four hours.
Was this productive use of my time, given that I could have achieved the same effect just by not including that data set and hoping customers didn't notice on their own?
This adds a bit of spit-and-polish my competitors in many cases lack and allows me to make use of more freebies than I would be able to otherwise.
Localizing Japanese to English and English to Japanese makes for some very fun times with anything that relies on any assumptions about physical width of strings, let me tell you!
The first chance you'll have to notice 'Hey, that looks funny' is when the word appears on the screen like 'Java decided to tr...'
This was pure pain to have to discover but I think its worth the price of admission for this week by itself.
key_insight: Investing hours fixing obscure edge cases in promotional materials is worthwhile because the resulting polish differentiates your product from competitors and expands what you can offer customers.
